conditionDescriptions: History of first trimester pregnancy exposure
contraindications.clinicalRationale: ACE inhibitors cause major congenital malformations when used during first trimester of pregnancy, as demonstrated in large epidemiological studies showing increased teratogenic risk
contraindications.riskDescription: Major congenital malformations in developing fetus
contraindications.alternativeConsiderations: Alternative antihypertensive agents safe in pregnancy should be considered for women of childbearing potential
contraindications.sourceSnippet: Cooper WO, Hernandez-Diaz S, Arbogast PG, et al. Major congenital malformations after rst-trimester exposure to ACE inhibitors. N Engl J Med. 2006;354:2443–2451.
